steps: Started a fresh Vite dev server at http://127.0.0.1:1420/.
Opened the app in Zen and selected Performance from the mode rail.
Confirmed the Performance workspace shows Live control surface with scenes, variations, save variation, macro controls, macro editor, hardware controls, and activity in one bounded cockpit viewport.
Confirmed the macro editor remains available as a bounded panel and does not dominate the default view.
Clicked Restore on the visible variation.
Focused the macro slider control and confirmed it remains in the compact macro strip.
Confirmed activity is summarized in the bottom strip/panel and hardware controls remain readable in the right column.

artifacts.label: performance control surface screenshot
artifacts.note: Captured from the visible Zen browser after opening Performance mode with the dense cockpit layout.

notes: Accessibility tree exposed Performance workspace, Live control surface, scenes, variations, macro controls, macro editor, hardware, and activity sections.
Existing automated tests plus build cover recall scene, save/restore variation, macro create/update/remove, and macro value action wiring through the store/backend paths.
